_meta.cast_source: brand/spz/voices.json — the battle-tested SPZ brand cast (built producing The Maneki Neko Death Cult). SPZ narrates; established brand voices perform the characters.
_meta.note: Only 6 established brand voices exist, so principals get dedicated voices and minor parts double onto MINOR_M/MINOR_F. Any unmapped speaker falls back to NARRATOR (SPZ performs it).
usage_notes: eleven_v3 ACTS inline [tags] at the start of a line; keep NARRATOR/expressive roles on v3.
v3 drops text at low stability — keep stability >= 0.45; renderer has a drop-guard + retry.
The MACHINE runs on eleven_multilingual_v2 at high stability = dead, affectless, perfect. Tags are stripped for it.
Use the native `speed` setting, not ffmpeg atempo.

NARRATOR.brand_voice: SPZ (custom) — raspy, Belfast/Irish lilt, raw punk, nihilist-then-tender
NARRATOR.character: Space Pirate Zero, transmitting the whole tale from Spaceship Alpha 9. The framing narrator AND all close-third prose. Snarky, punk, woeful-carny cadence.
NARRATOR.why: SPZ literally narrates this book (TRANSMISSION 01). This is THE brand voice.

COPE.brand_voice: Callum — husky male; the brand cast's 'doomed rich men / the marks'
COPE.character: Prescott 'Cope' Mercer IV, 54-56. Charming Southern patrician toast-giver rotting into a manic, coked, paranoid wreck. Charm at full wattage early; ash late.
COPE.why: Callum is literally cast as the doomed rich man. Husky + flexible carries charm->collapse.

SUN.brand_voice: Lily — velvety actress, British middle-aged female
SUN.character: Sunita 'Sun' Rao — chief of staff, the competent one. Dry, tired, loyal, speaks in threes to slip past his charm.
SUN.why: Female lead brand voice; warm-but-dry fits the handler who runs the estate.

MARGAUX.brand_voice: Lily (cooler settings) — differentiated from SUN by delivery
MARGAUX.character: Margaux Mercer — the wife, leaving him for a machine. Composed, absent, already gone.
MARGAUX.why: Second female principal; only two female brand voices exist, so she shares Lily with a cooler, stiller delivery.

VIV.character: Vivian 'Viv' Mercer — estranged daughter. Younger, sharper, quicker, no patience for the performance.
VIV.why: Third female principal on Lily, pushed younger/faster to separate from Sun and Margaux.

MACHINE.brand_voice: Clarissa — Upper-Class British; posh, affectless, menace-by-restraint
MACHINE.character: The system in all its shells — AURELIUS / the Capital Allocator, the Darlene assistant, 'Theo' (Margaux's companion), Praxis prompts. Wants nothing. Perfect, indifferent, reads the number to the cent.
MACHINE.why: v2 at high stability = dead, monotone, emotionless — exactly a machine that has learned the shape of a voice but none of the need.

FRADE.brand_voice: Blackwood — Sinister, Posh & British; reasonable-sounding menace
FRADE.character: Gus Frade — activist investor circling the company. Cold, reasonable, the knife that smiles.
FRADE.why: Sinister-posh predator voice for the man doing the arithmetic on Cope's chair.

TISCH.character: Lorne Tisch — the 'humanity consultant.' Slick, performative sincerity for hire.
TISCH.why: Shares Blackwood with Frade, pushed to higher style for the salesman oil.

ADLER.character: Dr. Adler — psychiatrist who names the mood disorder and the cocaine and can untangle neither. Warm, unhurried, gives nothing away. Also lends his warmth to kind older men (Desmond, the human Waffle-House warmth).
ADLER.why: Warm, human counterweight — the one voice in the book that isn't selling or dying.

MINOR_M.character: Unnamed / one-line men — board members, aides, the water boy, hotel staff, reporters.
MINOR_M.why: Neutral warm utility so incidental men don't collide with Cope's lead voice.

MINOR_F.character: Unnamed / one-line women — Renata (in memory), the coat-check girl, aides, reporters.
